item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've always loved insect skips haha, this one is not an exception. Well done!

Notably, SCP-4878 will not harm humans, even in self-defense.

I wish people understood this about cockroaches, all they want is defence from the rain or cold :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And the award for the least amount of words in the containment procedures goes to…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is good and I have upvoted. Feel good about your cockroach controller. An even better use for this thing would be to locate all cockroaches in a 700 m radius, gathering them in one spot for easy incineration.

Cockroach army is cool too, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sooner than you think1; after all, we're still in Phase 1.

Footnotes
1. Assuming you think it's coming out when the sun becomes a red giant

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Delightfully wacky.

So now you have a roach hivemind, time travelling tumour lady, skin monster, abundant-Arm-ando, Miss Dynamite, and I swear you wrote a dino-mech that one time. When is the Weryllium Cinematic Universe coming out, ft. all these weird, superpowered creatures?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The size is easy to fix, I'll just change the dimensions a bit or make it a part of a brain. Admittedly I didn't do as much math as I usually do.

As for the shape forming,

While in an SCP-4878-A state, affected cockroaches gain increased strength and resilience

is meant to be the handwave.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always love decrypting a puzzle from the future: why does this thing exist? What is its purpose? It's good to have clues to piece together and this entry does a good job with that. I also like that it's not a doomsday device — someone sent this from the future but I don't get the impression that it's the "last, best hope for humanity" or anything like that. Nope, just cockroach conscription.

Description: SCP-4878 is a small (35 cm long) ovoid, metallic signaling device that influences cockroach behavior.

Something about this line really grabbed me! Not sure why, but I was totally onboard from this point forward.

My only gripe is a small one, and it's about size.

+ show block
– hide block
  • There's a brain in this capsule, which is believable with it being ovoid and 35 cm long, but just barely (feels like it'd be cramped).
  • But then a group of cockroaches is strong enough to carry it?
  • I know it's nitpicky to try to cite physics on a site about unexplained phenomena, but the cockroaches forming lots of different shapes feels a little goofy and out of place. Is there a reason they don't collapse under their own weight? The article doesn't list that as one of their properties.

Minor complaints. If anyone needs me I'll be over in the corner, muttering about the Square-cube law.

item.title: BATTLE ROACHES
item.content:encoded:
Was starting to get worried I wasn't gonna get a skip out this month.

Thanks to BlueJones, DrChandra, ihp, and everyone else who took a look.1

New pic is CC 3.0 source here: https://commons.m.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cockroach_May_2007-1.jpg

Footnotes
1. Also thanks to OpenCircle for the name
